Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Book: The Emerging Science of Machine Learning Benchmarks 124 upvotes, 8 comments
  2. Afroman found not liable in defamation case brought by Ohio cops who raided home 37 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. OpenRocket 590 upvotes, 104 comments
  4. Show HN: I built 48 lightweight SVG backgrounds you can copy/paste 289 upvotes, 58 comments
  5. Nvidia greenboost: transparently extend GPU VRAM using system RAM/NVMe 369 upvotes, 83 comments
  6. Autoresearch for SAT Solvers 138 upvotes, 26 comments
  7. Wander – A tiny, decentralised tool to explore the small web 294 upvotes, 73 comments
  8. Conway's Game of Life, in real life 173 upvotes, 42 comments
  9. Cook: A simple CLI for orchestrating Claude Code 224 upvotes, 58 comments
  10. Why Cloudflare rule order matters? 44 upvotes, 7 comments
  11. Denmark was reportedly preparing for full-scale war with the US over Greenland 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. Show HN: Duplicate 3 layers in a 24B LLM, logical deduction .22→.76. No training 145 upvotes, 43 comments
  13. Rob Pike’s Rules of Programming (1989) 940 upvotes, 437 comments
  14. Czech Man's Stone in Barn's Foundations Is Rare Bronze Age Spearhead Mold 53 upvotes, 17 comments
  15. RX – a new random-access JSON alternative 103 upvotes, 40 comments
  16. A sufficiently detailed spec is code 418 upvotes, 216 comments
  17. Twelve-Tone Composition 9 upvotes, 1 comments
  18. LotusNotes 105 upvotes, 53 comments
  19. Warranty Void If Regenerated 379 upvotes, 230 comments
  20. The math that explains why bell curves are everywhere 147 upvotes, 85 comments
  21. Nvidia NemoClaw 328 upvotes, 217 comments
  22. Stdwin: Standard window interface by Guido Van Rossum [pdf] 35 upvotes, 16 comments
  23. Mozilla to launch free built-in VPN in upcoming Firefox 149 167 upvotes, 110 comments
  24. Show HN: Pano, a bookmarking tool built around shareable shelves 26 upvotes, 11 comments
  25. Show HN: Browser grand strategy game for hundreds of players on huge maps 39 upvotes, 20 comments
  26. 2% of ICML papers desk rejected because the authors used LLM in their reviews 91 upvotes, 62 comments
  27. Eniac, the First General-Purpose Digital Computer, Turns 80 28 upvotes, 17 comments
  28. OpenAI Has New Focus (on the IPO) 239 upvotes, 222 comments
  29. Austin’s surge of new housing construction drove down rents 591 upvotes, 690 comments
  30. Show HN: Will my flight have Starlink? 229 upvotes, 306 comments

Last generated 8:00pm, 19 March 2026. Source here.
